summary: This bundle records deterministic proof that the supported release smoke channel is `install_sh`, while docs point legacy Homebrew users to uninstall and reinstall through `install.sh`.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs claim that legacy Homebrew is not a supported update channel and should migrate to install.sh is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Cautilus can automatically migrate a live Homebrew installation
Homebrew formula metadata is inspected at runtime
Every user has already removed legacy Homebrew installs

notableSubtests: renderInstallerUrl points at the public install.sh path
runInstallSmoke drives the install.sh channel through an isolated workspace
parseArgs rejects legacy Homebrew as an install smoke channel
install.sh downloads a tagged binary asset and writes a wrapper that preserves caller context

decision.reason: The release smoke runner accepts only `install_sh`, the focused test rejects `homebrew`, and both user-facing and maintainer-facing docs direct legacy Homebrew users to uninstall and reinstall through install.sh.
